title: From-scratch rewrite of authentication guard and inventory add-item functionality

requirements.summary: Completely rewrite the authentication guard in App.tsx from scratch to synchronously check localStorage session on every protected route render and immediately redirect to /login if no valid session exists
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app with no session in localStorage always lands on /login
Navigating directly to /dashboard, /inventory, /billing, or /reports without a session redirects to /login
After logging in, navigating to /login redirects to /dashboard
After logout, all protected routes redirect to /login
No flicker or momentary render of a protected page before redirect

file_operations.description: Rewrite the authentication guard from scratch. Remove the existing beforeLoad auth logic. Implement a new synchronous session check that reads from localStorage on every render of protected routes. Ensure the root '/' route redirects to /login when no session exists and to /dashboard when authenticated. Ensure /login redirects to /dashboard when already authenticated. Configure the protected layout route to use a beforeLoad guard that synchronously reads the session flag from localStorage and immediately redirects to /login if the flag is missing or invalid, preventing any protected component from mounting.

requirements.summary: Completely rewrite the Add Item form on the Inventory page from scratch to collect all required fields, validate inputs, call addItem mutation with correct argument shape, display notifications, clear form, and invalidate inventory query
acceptanceCriteria: Filling in all fields and clicking Add Item successfully creates the item on the backend
The new item appears in the inventory table immediately after submission without a manual page refresh
Submitting with any field empty shows an inline validation error and does not call the backend
A success toast or notification is displayed after a successful add
The form resets to empty state after a successful submission
If the backend returns an error, a clear error message is displayed to the user

file_operations.description: Completely rewrite the Add Item form from scratch. Remove all existing add-item form logic. Implement a new controlled form with four fields: Item Name (text input, required), Category (select dropdown using categories from frontend/src/lib/constants.ts, required), Quantity (number input for positive integers, required), and Price per Item (number input for positive numbers, required). Add inline validation that checks all fields are filled before enabling submit. On submit, call the useAddItem mutation hook with arguments matching the backend interface signature: addItem(name: string, category: ItemCategory, quantity: bigint, pricePerItem: number). After successful mutation, display a success toast notification, clear all form fields to empty state, and explicitly invalidate the inventory query using queryClient.invalidateQueries so the InventoryTable re-fetches and displays the new item. Handle mutation errors by displaying a clear error message to the user. Verify the useAddItem hook from frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts returns the correct mutation shape before implementing.
